what_it_does: This is a pnpm lockfile (lockfileVersion 9.0) for the canvas/app package. It records the exact resolved versions of direct and transitive dependencies, the dependency graph, peer dependencies, optional platform-specific packages, and integrity hashes so that installs are reproducible.
observations: Root importer depends on react@18.3.1, react-dom@18.3.1, and tldraw@3.15.6 (resolved from ^3.13.1).
Dev dependencies include @vitejs/plugin-react@4.7.0, typescript@5.9.3, and vite@6.4.2.
A large transitive tree includes the full Radix UI component suite, TipTap editor packages, and @tldraw/* internals.
Many @esbuild and @rollup platform packages are listed as optional dependencies.
The snapshots section spells out the resolved dependency graph, including peer-dependency wiring such as react-dom@18.3.1(react@18.3.1).
standards: The file follows pnpm v9 lockfile structure with importers, packages, and snapshots sections.
Semver ranges in the importer are resolved to exact versions in packages/snapshots.
Peer dependencies are rendered with parenthetical instantiation, e.g. react-dom@18.3.1(react@18.3.1).
concerns: lodash.isequal@4.5.0 is marked deprecated in the lockfile metadata.
tldraw resolved to 3.15.6 while the specifier is ^3.13.1, which is normal but means the lockfile is the source of truth for the actual version.
The dependency surface is large (many Radix UI and TipTap packages), increasing audit and update scope.
notes: This lockfile anchors a React + Vite + TypeScript app built around tldraw, with Radix UI primitives and TipTap rich-text editing pulled in transitively.
intention_signals: The file is trying to freeze a working dependency tree for a tldraw-based React application so that every install resolves the same package versions and peer-dependency pairings.
contribution: It contributes a deterministic, integrity-checked dependency resolution graph that downstream tooling can use for reproducible installs, security audits, and update planning.
summary_for_embedding: pnpm lockfile pinning exact versions of React, tldraw, Radix UI, TipTap, Vite, and their transitive dependencies for the canvas/app package.
intention_for_embedding: Record the resolved dependency tree so installs are reproducible and consistent across environments.
